name: Karen Darkes, Emerge
subject: Celebrating Black Emerge alums and inspiring change this Black History Month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emerge America This email was sent by Emerge America
body:
| | | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/78302013/452993115/-1986725772?amounts=10,20,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1416-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5MmQ0Yzk2LTI4YzItZWUxMS1iNjYwLTAwMjI0ODIyMzE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jBo0H6jvtAc8iMX-v7OQoeSfsE70YxLdyAmQlRjiWxE=)
---
Peter,
As we embark on the celebration of Black History Month, I am thrilled to shine a spotlight on two incredible Black Emerge alums who exemplify resilience, leadership, and a steadfast commitment to their communities.
I’m excited to share their stories with you – but first, I have an urgent ask:
**[We're looking for supporters like you to recruit and train more Black women leaders to take on positions with real decision-making power. In honor of Black History Month, will you give $10 or more right now to help more Black women run for office and win in every state?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/78302014/452993143/-1986725772?amounts=10,20,50,100,250&amount=10&refcode=jtk1416-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5MmQ0Yzk2LTI4YzItZWUxMS1iNjYwLTAwMjI0ODIyMzE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jBo0H6jvtAc8iMX-v7OQoeSfsE70YxLdyAmQlRjiWxE=)**
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/78302015/452993144/-1986725772?amounts=10,20,50,100,250&amount=25&refcode=jtk1416-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5MmQ0Yzk2LTI4YzItZWUxMS1iNjYwLTAwMjI0ODIyMzE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jBo0H6jvtAc8iMX-v7OQoeSfsE70YxLdyAmQlRjiWxE=)**Tameika Isaac Devine,** a trailblazing Black woman, marks Emerge's first triumph of the year. Tameika has consistently demonstrated her dedication to public service and community empowerment. As a member of Emerge’s Black Leadership Circle and our second Seated Together cohort to help advance Black elected officials, she has made it her life’s work to protect people, neighborhoods, and our rights. **_And on Jan. 2, she won her race for South Carolina state Senate!_**
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/78302016/452993145/-1986725772?amounts=10,20,50,100,250&amount=25&refcode=jtk1416-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5MmQ0Yzk2LTI4YzItZWUxMS1iNjYwLTAwMjI0ODIyMzE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jBo0H6jvtAc8iMX-v7OQoeSfsE70YxLdyAmQlRjiWxE=)**City Councilmember Carolyn Wysinger** is another luminary rising from Emerge's second Seated Together cohort and the first out LGBTQ+ Black woman elected to her seat. And this year, the city council elected her as Mayor Pro Tem of El Cerrito, California. Her unwavering dedication to her community has paved the way for historic accomplishments, and her story is a testament to the impact of Emerge's programs in fostering diverse leadership.
In this special month dedicated to honoring Black history, I’m thrilled to celebrate the achievements of these two women dedicated to defeating the GOP's agenda in 2024 and beyond.
**But to keep supporting Democratic wins with our unique training and recruitment programs that empower women to run, we need your help.[Please, in honor of Black History Month, will you donate $10 or more now to help Emerge recruit, train, and empower MORE Democratic women to run for office and WIN in 2024?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/78302017/452993146/-1986725772?amounts=10,20,50,100,250&amount=25&refcode=jtk1416-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5MmQ0Yzk2LTI4YzItZWUxMS1iNjYwLTAwMjI0ODIyMzE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jBo0H6jvtAc8iMX-v7OQoeSfsE70YxLdyAmQlRjiWxE=)**
Thank you for joining us in recognizing these remarkable women and their contributions to our communities.

Karen Darkes
Major Gifts Director
Emerge
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/78302018/452993147/-1986725772?amounts=10,20,50,100,250&amount=25&refcode=jtk1416-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImM5MmQ0Yzk2LTI4YzItZWUxMS1iNjYwLTAwMjI0ODIyMzE5NyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=jBo0H6jvtAc8iMX-v7OQoeSfsE70YxLdyAmQlRjiWxE=)
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
Email is an important way for Emerge America to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. Questions or concerns? [Contact us here.](mailto:connect@emergeamerica.org) Paid for by Emerge America This email was sent by Emerge America

subject: Donald Trump goes after MSNBC!

body:
| [ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/26910?t=1&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
---
**Donald Trump said he WILL investigate media outlets like _MSNBC_ for TREASON if he retakes the White House in 2024!** _He thinks they should be PUNISHED for following the evidence and reporting truthfully about our democratic elections!_
* * *
**So we’re conducting an _URGENT_ poll to see how Democrats feel about news outlets performance under the pressure of Trump’s _RELENTLESS ATTACKS:_**

**[_How would you grade _MSNBC_?_](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=7&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)** [ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=8&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D) | [A: ABSOLUTELY TERRIFIC! »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=9&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
---
[B: GREAT! »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=10&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
[C: ADEQUATE »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=11&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
[D: UNSATISFACTORY »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=12&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
[F: TERRIBLE »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=13&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
****__500 POLL RESPONSES NEEDED BY 11:59 PM__ TO MAKE THIS POLL VIABLE FOR REFERENCE IN OUR 2024 ELECTION STRATEGY. _ONLY MAGA REPUBLICANS HAVE GIVEN MSNBC AN F RATING AS OF YET._ ****
* * *
_Donald Trump and his MAGA army are trying to make voters lose faith in every last democratic institution!_

**...and media watchdogs like _MSNBC_ are just their latest target!**

We need to know that their plan to overtake this country is NOT impacting the minds of our most loyal Democratic supporters!

[_**So PLEASE: submit your poll response quickly! »**_](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/grade_msnbc?source=FFR_20240202_EMPOLL_E3_T0_AC&t=14&akid=20719%2E4449941%2E972N6D)
**500 POLL RESPONSES NEEDED BY 11:59 PM**
Thank you for your commitment to upholding democracy,

-FFR Polling Team
_MSNBC_ is not associated or affiliated with Fight for Reform and has not sponsored or approved of this communication.
---

name: Dom Jones for Assembly 2024
subject: Video Alert! 📽️ Dive into February Festivities with Dom Jones – 33 Days of Rallies, Fun, and More!
body:
Dive right in! We have 33 days left until the Primary of March 5th and our team is ready and excited for Democracy!
| | **Can't see this message?_[View in a browser](https://www.domjones.org/so/52Ori5tya/c?w=A1AmzgCHhYJADMFsaqCZMJdg6NkL86HQ2ShLO3FLpuo.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG9tam9uZXMub3JnL3NvLzUyT3JpNXR5YT9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjUxYmIxNzQ0LTJhOTMtNDMwYy04YzQ3LTA4MDY4MDZkZDFjMiJ9)_**

| | | [](https://www.domjones.org/so/52Ori5tya/c?w=N5x4a2XgXOixeV39eyq_1LkmPSCDFeB8nT1WezZigoA.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG9tam9uZXMub3JnIiwiciI6ImIxYTdkZGRjLWRiOWUtNDAzZC03N2NhLWIwODBkMGFiZDgwOCIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjUxYmIxNzQ0LTJhOTMtNDMwYy04YzQ3LTA4MDY4MDZkZDFjMiJ9)

| | This message can be viewed in a browser and shared, please click the link above the newsletter, that says"view in browser" and share. :-)
---
| | FebruaryEvents!This WeekendWE KICK OFF THE MONTH!
---
| | Explore the upcoming events integrated into our campaign trajectory! With a mere 33 days remaining until the pivotal election day on March 5th, our collective efforts must intensify within the community. It is imperative that we convey the indisputable fact:Dom Jones stands not just as the optimal choice for representation as our next Assemblywoman, but as the sole contender in this race endowed with the prowess to usher in unparalleled economic prosperity for Coastal Orange County. If you want to hear how, come hear the moving words of Dom Jones at one of our campaign rallies mentioned below! Dom Jones embodies the rare convergence of qualities that delineates her as the once-in-a-lifetime candidate. She not only exemplifies the pinnacle of capability but also illuminates the realm of possibilities within our grasp. Regardless of political affiliations, be it Republican, Democrat, or Independent, the resounding truth persists – Dom Jones stands as the beacon of progress and prosperity that our community deserves. It is time to rally behind a candidate who not only envisions a brighter future but actively propels us toward its realization.
---
| | JOIN US SATURDAY FEBRUARY 3RD & 4TH!
---
| |
---
| | WATCH THE VIDEO!DOM SHARES WHAT'S NEXT!
---
| | [](https://www.domjones.org/so/52Ori5tya/c?w=niX1CPO25gOxHuWNQ0bMbUgL1s7Qsk7kW9BBGCDKkHo.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG9tam9uZXMub3JnL3NvLzUyT3JpNXR5YT9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsInIiOiIwODdlZjQ4ZS1lNjliLTRlZTEtYTljMi0zMjdhZWU3MmQ4YmQiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI1MWJiMTc0NC0yYTkzLTQzMGMtOGM0Ny0wODA2ODA2ZGQxYzIifQ)
---
| |
---
| |
---
| | Be mindful of the current path set by the Huntington Beach City Council, steering our communities toward increased division, disharmony, and the exclusion of various Huntington Beach residents. Delve into the details of the HB Ballot measures, where the Dom Jones community passionately advocates for a resounding "NO" vote. Visit [protecthb.org](https://www.domjones.org/so/52Ori5tya/c?w=SFXaVOmTfE1ajwJzPgNPMqGjVU-28QID2-rdyVqehcI.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm90ZWN0aGIub3JnIiwiciI6IjAwYmFjOTEyLWRlNDAtNDU5OC05YmE3LWEyMTg0MjBlMDkxOCIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjUxYmIxNzQ0LTJhOTMtNDMwYy04YzQ3LTA4MDY4MDZkZDFjMiJ9) by clicking on the image below or following the embedded link here to acquaint yourself with the ongoing efforts to safeguard Huntington Beach. This is not merely a political maneuver; it is a vital initiative to preserve the cohesion of our city with the county, fostering the collective well-being of all community members. Let's actively engage in city council meetings, presenting a united front against the divisive measures proposed. Together, let us champion UNITY and INCLUSION, transcending political affiliations and standing as one harmonious voice for the betterment of Huntington Beach.
---
| | [](https://www.domjones.org/so/52Ori5tya/c?w=blqqB4a7H5GFBFBbWH9fYDwTfITrHTvLUocWuvjSvDg.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wcm90ZWN0aGIub3JnIiwiciI6IjliODdiYzhmLThlNWYtNDIxMS05ZTcyLWZjMzM1MmJkM2ViZiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjUxYmIxNzQ0LTJhOTMtNDMwYy04YzQ3LTA4MDY4MDZkZDFjMiJ9)

This is not just a campaign; it's a movement, and she needs YOU to help us amplify her message! Running a grassroots campaign isn't a walk in the park, but it's a stride towards a historic victory that will resonate for generations. 🚀🎉 Your donation isn't just a contribution; it's a catalyst for change. Together, let's break barriers, shatter ceilings, and rewrite the narrative! In this pivotal moment, your support is the fuel that propels Dom Jones forward. body:

name: Katie Porter
subject: Watch: Our second TV ad calls out the BS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
Look, I’m in this race not to be wined and dined by lobbyists, but to fight for California’s future.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=37237%2E3422442%2EZ8KzIn)
Derek, our second TV ad is live! **[Click here to watch for yourself.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20240202-2?refcode=em20240202-37237&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=37237_3422442_Z8KzIn&akid=37237%2E3422442%2EZ8KzIn)**

Look, I’m in this race not to be wined and dined by lobbyists, but to fight for California’s future. To build affordable housing, un-rig our economy, and take on the climate crisis, **we need to actually stand up to special interests—not just point fingers and cash corporate checks.**
I can only make it to the U.S. Senate with your help. **[Can you pitch in right now to help us run this TV ad?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20240202-2?refcode=em20240202-37237&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=37237_3422442_Z8KzIn&akid=37237%2E3422442%2EZ8KzIn)**

Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=em20240202-37237&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=37237_3422442_Z8KzIn&akid=37237%2E3422442%2EZ8KzIn)**

name: Mucarsel-Powell, Debbie
subject: I want to tell you a story
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Debbie for Florida
body:
how donuts are going to help us save our Senate majority 🍩
| | |
---
Friend, I wanted to reach out again before the end of the day because we have an important deadline coming up. I'm about to ask you to help me hit my $25,000 fundraising goal. But first, I want to tell you a story about donuts — and how they're going to help us save our Senate majority.
**[(If you're short on time and ready to retire Rick Scott this fall, you can chip in here to help me make up this fundraising gap and flip Florida blue.)](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78302197/452997676/849749119?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk746-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjhjZTBmZmMtMjljMi1lZTExLWI2NjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzMTk3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ofW3K4B8f1b5thk5k6PatjwOpjpddzYXWc9siPu9LDw=)**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78302198/452997677/849749119?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk746-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjhjZTBmZmMtMjljMi1lZTExLWI2NjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzMTk3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ofW3K4B8f1b5thk5k6PatjwOpjpddzYXWc9siPu9LDw=)
When I was 14, my family came to America to seek a better life. We arrived here with big dreams for the future. But those early days were hard. I remember my mom working back-to-back double shifts and _still_ struggling to afford rent for our small, one room apartment.
**So, after I turned 15, I got a job working the early morning shift at a local donut shop.** It was just a minimum wage job, but the small checks I brought home gave my family room to breathe. Finally, after a lot of hard work, the possibilities that we could only dream of before became reality.
We were able to move out of that one room apartment, and I got a scholarship to an incredible college. Eventually, I found a career path where I could give back to the communities and country that gave me so much. That call to service took me to places I had never even dreamed of before. **I became the first South American immigrant to serve in Congress, and now, I'm running for the United States Senate.**
**Making donuts in the morning before homeroom brought me all the way to Congress. ****I've seen time and time again how small acts have the power to create big, transformative change._And that's exactly how we're going to win this campaign in November._**
[**That's why I'm asking you to chip in just $10 — about the cost of a dozen donuts — to send me to the Senate. Can you chip in before midnight to help me meet my daily fundraising goal and defeat Rick Scott? clean_body: how donuts are going to help us save our Senate majority 🍩 | | | Friend, I wanted to reach out again before the end of the day because we have an important deadline coming up. I'm about to ask you to help me hit my $25,000 fundraising goal. But first, I want to tell you a story about donuts — and how they're going to help us save our Senate majority. = = When I was 14, my family came to America to seek a better life. We arrived here with big dreams for the future. But those early days were hard. I remember my mom working back-to-back double shifts and still struggling to afford rent for our small, one room apartment. So, after I turned 15, I got a job working the early morning shift at a local donut shop. It was just a minimum wage job, but the small checks I brought home gave my family room to breathe. Finally, after a lot of hard work, the possibilities that we could only dream of before became reality. We were able to move out of that one room apartment, and I got a scholarship to an incredible college. Eventually, I found a career path where I could give back to the communities and country that gave me so much. That call to service took me to places I had never even dreamed of before. I became the first South American immigrant to serve in Congress, and now, I'm running for the United States Senate. Making donuts in the morning before homeroom brought me all the way to Congress. I've seen time and time again how small acts have the power to create big, transformative change.And that's exactly how we're going to win this campaign in November. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thank you so much, Debbie Mucarsel-Powell | * | | | = Debbie Mucarsel-Powell is a Floridian, South American immigrant, mother, and former Congresswoman. She’s dedicated her life to uplifting working families, ending gun violence, and fighting for her community. Now, she’s running to defeat Republican Senator Rick Scott and win a better, brighter future for Florida.

name: Conservative Broadcast
subject: C.S. Lewis’s Profound Insights
body:
His signature ability to communicate the truth about Christianity has inspired countless skeptics

This sponsored message from Conservative Broadcast is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | | [](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/sIPgE86EStTSfpQSd7Dz_Q~~/AARclAA~~~)
---
| | Dear Friend, C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

His signature ability to communicate the truth about Christianity has inspired countless skeptics to become believers and even more believers to renew and deepen their faith--making him the greatest Christian apologist of the twentieth century.

That's why we produced "[**C.S. Lewis on Christianity,**](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/sIPgE86EStTSfpQSd7Dz_Q~~/AARclAA~~~)" which you can now own on DVD.
---
| [](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/sIPgE86EStTSfpQSd7Dz_Q~~/AARclAA~~~)
---
| | In this course you will learn Lewis's profound insights on the central topics of Christianity, including:
* good and evil,
* conversion and faith,
* prayer and the Bible,
* suffering and death,
* heaven and hell, and much more.

Dr. Ward is a Distinguished Visiting Professor at Hillsdale College and a member of the Faculty of Theology and Religion at the University of Oxford. He is the author of _Planet Narnia: The Seven Heavens in the Imagination of C.S. Lewis_ and _After Humanity: A Guide to C.S. Lewis 's The Abolition of Man_, and the co-editor of _The Cambridge Companion to C.S. Lewis_. He even spent three years living in Lewis's Oxford home as the resident Warden of The Kilns!

This course is especially moving because it examines C.S. Lewis's beautiful writings about faith and traces Lewis's own journey from skeptic to believer. The combination provides an opportunity for us to grow in our knowledge and understanding of Christianity.

That's why I hope you'll join us in examining the eternal questions of Christianity with C.S. Lewis with [this beautiful DVD box set](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/sIPgE86EStTSfpQSd7Dz_Q~~/AARclAA~~~).

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
---
| |
---

Conservative Broadcast
---
_PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522_

name: Dr. Brian Williams
subject: Rising above
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Brian Williams for Congress
body:
| | Peter,

My opponent in the race to represent TX-32 has repeatedly disparaged my service, intelligence, and dedication to this community. As a Black man, I’ve always risen above such attempts to diminish my worth over my decades of service. So once again, I took the high road and focused on why I’m running and how it contrasts with her voting record.

But her most recent attack has gone too far. She's trying to deceive voters and has called pointing out her votes, "Trump-style attacks." As your Congressman, I will never lie to you or misrepresent facts for my convenience — excellence, integrity, and service before self are the pillars of my Air Force training I carry with me.

And the facts here are undeniable: My primary opponent's record speaks for itself. But since she continues to deny, deflect, and deceive, let’s talk about the facts my team laid out in a recent email.

clean_body: | | Peter, My opponent in the race to represent TX-32 has repeatedly disparaged my service, intelligence, and dedication to this community. As a Black man, I’ve always risen above such attempts to diminish my worth over my decades of service. So once again, I took the high road and focused on why I’m running and how it contrasts with her voting record. But her most recent attack has gone too far. She's trying to deceive voters and has called pointing out her votes, "Trump-style attacks." As your Congressman, I will never lie to you or misrepresent facts for my convenience — excellence, integrity, and service before self are the pillars of my Air Force training I carry with me. And the facts here are undeniable: My primary opponent's record speaks for itself. But since she continues to deny, deflect, and deceive, let’s talk about the facts my team laid out in a recent email. , , , , , and : The six votes where Julie Johnson sided with the NRA to weaken gun laws. These bills banned local communities from setting their own ordinances around the sale or transfer of guns or ammunition, repealed restrictions on silencers, and designated gun shops as essential businesses during state emergencies. With just over four weeks until the primary, this will not be the last time I’ll have to defend my experience and qualifications as a candidate for Congress. But we will continue the fight because my campaign is fueled by purpose, our movement is strong, and we have the momentum to win the primary on March 5. subject: Now is the time to rally behind President Biden
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
This is no time to sit on the sidelines.
| | |
---
| | **FIVETHIRTYEIGHT POLLING UPDATE:**
---
**Trump and Biden are neck and neck in the polls!**
---
**We need to rally behind President Biden before it's too late — _but we still don't know where you stand!_**
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78302735/453012324/-135443309?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=43614&ask=10&amounts=5,25,50,100&code=jtk4777-su-nd&refcode=jtk4777-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwM2E0MTdlMC0yZWMyLWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=I0OfIvJSZGMehclsnbD8iLD9UkFzAnDzFm7tyPdc6Iw=&emci=d5ee4159-e7c1-ee11-b660-002248223197&emdi=03a417e0-2ec2-ee11-b660-002248223197&ceid=7659029)
---
**_Please respond before 11:59 p.m. to ensure your response is counted._**
---
Friend: From improving our nation’s infrastructure and helping create millions of new jobs to lowering health care costs and making college more affordable, President Biden has delivered real progress for the American people.
But Republicans have been working overtime to attack the president and undermine all of his accomplishments. **Now, polls show him locked in a statistical TIE with Donald Trump in the race for the White House!**
This is _dangerous_ _,_ friend. Trump has already attacked our democracy, and he’s racked up a staggering 91 criminal charges since leaving office. He even said that he will be a **_dictator_** on “day one” if reelected.

| Hello, I hope this message finds you well. I'm thrilled to share some exciting news about Gabe Evans' campaign for Congress. In case you missed it, Gabe made waves in 2023,**bringing in over $270,000** in support of his vision for our community. This significant milestone is a testament to the growing momentum behind Gabe's campaign. **Why Gabe Evans?**
1. **Proven Fundraising Power:** Gabe's ability to secure over $270,000 demonstrates widespread support for his conservative principles and dedication to our community. Gabe out raised his primary opponent in the fourth quarter by 4 to 1.
2. **National Traction:** Gabe is gaining major traction across the country, emerging as a front-runner in the race. People nationwide are recognizing the value of his leadership - including **Sen. Tom Cotton, Reps. Jack Bergman, Tony Gonzales, August Pfluger, and Brian Mast.**
3. **Straw Poll Success:** Gabe recently won the most recent straw poll in Weld County by over double the margin, showcasing the local enthusiasm for his candidacy.

**Join the Movement Today:** As we head into this crucial election year, it's clear that Gabe Evans is the candidate to rally behind. His background as a 22 year military and law enforcement veteran, coupled with his impressive fundraising, national recognition, and local support make him the front-runner in this race. **Together, we will win CO-8!** **How You Can Join the Movement:**
1. **Donate Now:** Contribute to Gabe's campaign to ensure we have the resources needed to secure victory. Visit [www.ElectGabeEvans.com](https://wayrap.clicks.mlsend.com/te/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjYwMjExMCxcImxcIjoxMTIxMTU5MTkzMzE3ODgyODIsXCJyXCI6MTEyMTE1OTIwNTExNDM2MzczfSIsInMiOiJjZDI0ODY4NWIzNWU0YTI3In0) to make a secure online donation.
2. **Spread the Word:** Share this email with friends, family, and fellow conservatives who want to support a proven leader. Encourage them to join the movement today.
3. **Stay Informed:** Follow Gabe on social media to stay updated on campaign events, news, and opportunities to get involved. Your engagement is crucial.

Gabe Evans is the candidate who will bring our shared values to Congress, and with your support, we can make it happen. Join the movement today and be a part of the team that secures a better future for our community. Thank you for your continued support. name: Tom Fitton
subject: NEW Fani Willis Lawsuit
body:
Ashli Babbitt Update

[INSIDE JW]

Judicial Watch Sues for Details of DA Willis’ Hiring of Special
Prosecutor

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31266&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdmlkZW9zL2NvbmZpcm1lZC1mYW5pLXdpbGxpcy1hZmZhaXIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=5ef3f99d2dac8458c03b2f1a57428e9547021181226f50211e5e8198a6d5bfb2]
We are in court fighting the lawfare being waged against former
President Donald Trump.

We filed a lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31250&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZnVsdG9uLWNvLWdhLXdhZGUtY29tcGxhaW50Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=f12a6768212a9452d79cfaf2beb1d5ff3a315894b7e8d210f859cdc9d2eb6af0]
against Fulton County, Georgia, for records of the hiring of Nathan
Wade as a special prosecutor by District Attorney Fani Willis
(_Judicial Watch Inc. v. Fulton County, Georgia_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31250&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZnVsdG9uLWNvLWdhLXdhZGUtY29tcGxhaW50Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=f12a6768212a9452d79cfaf2beb1d5ff3a315894b7e8d210f859cdc9d2eb6af0]).

Wade was hired to pursue unprecedented criminal investigations and
prosecutions against former President Trump and others over the 2020
election disputes. We sued after Fulton County failed to respond to a
January 11, 2024, Georgia Open Records Act request for:

> All County public records, as defined in O.C.G.A.§ 50-18- 70(b)(2),
> related to the hiring/appointment/procurement of the professional
> services of Nathan Wade (or his law firm) as Fulton County Special
> Prosecutor. This includes any request for services/proposals,
> contracts, invoices, or correspondence (physical or electronic)
> related to his hiring/appointment/procurement.
>
> Any applicable procurement policies and procedures related to Mr.
> Wade’s hiring or appointment.
Wade and Willis are allegedly involved in a romantic relationship.
Earlier this month, records relating to Wade’s divorce were unsealed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31252&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9ueXBvc3QuY29tLzIwMjQvMDEvMjIvbmV3cy9kaXZvcmNlLWZpbGUtYXQtY2VudGVyLW9mLWZhbmktd2lsbGlzLXJlbGF0aW9uc2hpcC1jbGFpbXMtcmVsZWFzZWQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=a3a85fc698e80e6df5a8660d7fa4903e2c0508a36e6ae4999e652b9a30bcfc05]
by a judge. They included evidence of his relationship with Willis
such as “credit card charges for trips the pair took together to
Florida and California.” His wife’s attorneys also asserted that
Wade hid his true earnings, “totaling almost $700,000 for his work
as special prosecutor.”

Fani Willis’ politicized and unprecedented prosecution of former
President Trump has been further compromised by credible allegations
of personal corruption tied to the hiring of Nathan Wade as special
prosecutor. That we had to file a lawsuit to try to get records about
this scandal further suggests that there is something to hide.

In October 2023, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31253&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvd3AtY29udGVudC91cGxvYWRzLzIwMjMvMTAvSlctdi4tRE9KLUZ1bHRvbi1Dby1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDMxMTAucGRmP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=285c95128addde2a7429a6a960de93429284999124f2991eac12241a3f84741a]
the U.S. Department of Justice for records and communications between
the Office of U.S. Special Counsel Jack Smith and the Fulton County,
Georgia, District Attorney’s office regarding requests/receipt of
federal funding/assistance in the investigation of former President
Donald Trump and his 18 co-defendants in the Fulton County indictment
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31254&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY2JzbmV3cy5jb20vbmV3cy9yZWFkLXRydW1wLWdlb3JnaWEtaW5kaWN0bWVudC10ZXh0LWRvY3VtZW50LXBkZi1jb3B5LWNoYXJnZXMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=80f32275c3bd587af7eae8b47d63f5cc5c97d38f5077d96754b49c5eece589f5]
of August 14, 2023.

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FBI FOR FILES ON ASHLI BABBITT – FAMILY DEMANDS
RECORDS

Ashli Babbitt was the only homicide victim on January 6, yet the FBI
has been illicitly hiding its files on her from her family for a year.
Why the cover-up?

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31267&F=T&&hash=2bc55caad0025de3787a4b68834fbb69db9b52d42c6702cddf995e68a84a5661
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31263&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2VzdGF0ZS1vZi1hc2hsaS1iYWJiaXR0LWFuZC1hYXJvbi1iYWJiaXR0LXYtZG9qLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMTE5Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=6b01ec4b61a8cd420a999168b85849a64e548b6262a381975008d5f4a03e7695]
on behalf of Aaron Babbitt, the late Ashli Elizabeth Babbitt’s
husband, and Ashli Babbitt’s estate against the U.S. Department of
Justice (DOJ) for all FBI files on Ashli Babbitt
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31268&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2VzdGF0ZS1vZi1hc2hsaS1iYWJiaXR0LWFuZC1hYXJvbi1iYWJiaXR0LXYtZG9qLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMTE5L10oP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=64ad8f2ea520b69082cf5dff9b5da9d6792495fbc50e590db92f3767e9a2018b
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31269&F=T&&hash=e4fa63d4d2007e0280f2f049a0e27fb157cd6e5a336bd2d4961940ed5207c475
of Ashli Babbitt and Aaron Babbitt et al. v. U.S. Department of
Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31263&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2VzdGF0ZS1vZi1hc2hsaS1iYWJiaXR0LWFuZC1hYXJvbi1iYWJiaXR0LXYtZG9qLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMTE5Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=6b01ec4b61a8cd420a999168b85849a64e548b6262a381975008d5f4a03e7695]
(No. 24-cv-0119)).

Ashli Babbitt, an Air Force veteran, was shot and killed inside the
U.S. Capitol by then-Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd on January 6,
2021.

We sued in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of
California to compel production of the FBI files on Ashli Babbitt and
Aaron Babbitt after the FBI denied two February 27, 2023, FOIA
requests to the FBI and subsequently failed to respond to an appeal of
the denial for:

> All FBI files for Ashli Elizabeth Babbitt. Additional information:
> “Subject also used the names” Ashli Elizabeth McEntee and Ashli
> Elizabeth Pamatian;” and
>
> All FBI records, including main and cross-referenced files, related
> to Aaron Raymond Babbitt.
Babbitt was a 35-year-old resident of San Diego, California, where
she owned and operated a successful pool business with her husband
Aaron. Ashli traveled alone from San Diego to Washington, D.C. to
attend the Women for America First (aka Save America) rally on January
6, 2021, at the Ellipse.

The shooting occurred at the east entrance to the Speaker’s Lobby.
After demonstrators filled the hallway outside the lobby, two
individuals in the crowded, tightly packed hallway struck and
dislodged the glass panels in the lobby doors and the right door
sidelight. Lt. Byrd, who is a U.S. Capitol Police commander and was
the incident commander for the House on January 6, 2021, shot Ashli on
sight as she raised herself up into the opening of the right door
sidelight. Lt. Byrd later confessed that he shot Ashli before seeing
her hands or assessing her intentions or even identifying her as
female. Ashli was unarmed. Her hands were up in the air, empty, and in
plain view of Lt. Byrd and other officers in the lobby.

On January 5, 2024, we filed a wrongful death
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31270&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvd3JvbmdmdWwtZGVhdGgtbGF3c3VpdC1iYWJiaXR0L11sYXdzdWl0P3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=355923b72aa5aa16d40b7f4af9672e27cbc81d12b4ef93471a2881721527a062
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31027&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvd3JvbmdmdWwtZGVhdGgtbGF3c3VpdC1iYWJiaXR0Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=584699017f06cd66f0b59fcd03620a8c2836650fda2beee9b855a29384c8c2b9]
against the U.S. Government on behalf of the family of Ashli Babbitt
(_Estate of Ashli Babbitt and Aaron Babbitt, et al. v. United States
of America_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31015&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2VzdGF0ZS1vZi1hc2hsaS1iYWJiaXR0LWFuZC1hcnJvbi1iYWJiaXR0LWV0LWFsLXYtdS1zLWEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=3f8be2d18e75652be7156db169064f46f82feea9c5681837d885f5c69d1378f1]
(No. 3:24-cv-00033)). The lawsuit includes $30 million in claims
against the U.S. Government for wrongful death, assault and battery,
and various negligence issues. Describing Babbitt’s murder by Lt.
Byrd, our complaint recounts:

> The facts speak truth. Ashli was ambushed when she was shot by Lt.
> Byrd. Multiple witnesses at the scene yelled, “you just murdered
> her.”
>
> Lt. Byrd was never charged or otherwise punished or disciplined for
> Ashli’s homicide.
We are extensively investigating the events of January 6.

In October 2023, we
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31271&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvamFudWFyeS02L11hbm5vdW5jZWQ/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ff7f1c2953b248529c178140b9979d44d89b1d09d950700ca7901f81ad3fcd52
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=30448&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvamFudWFyeS02Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=a77953b8f6362a426fe92288d2bcedc210cb7e2efed6c897ce678d949b2e18ac]
that we received the
court-ordered
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31272&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdXMtY2FwaXRvbC1wb2xpY2UtZGVjbGFyYXRpb24tamFtZXMtdy1qb3ljZS0wMDQwMS9dZGVjbGFyYXRpb24/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=3e72ba393d745eb4ff1c7e3039cba8789d44b67c58b4d0f0f122d3600c327f1f
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=30428&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdXMtY2FwaXRvbC1wb2xpY2UtZGVjbGFyYXRpb24tamFtZXMtdy1qb3ljZS0wMDQwMS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=48268c79fb3ebf553b981bfa02ef3ab2fb0b9b8530a642d4293530034a35b709]
of James W. Joyce, senior counsel in the Office of the General Counsel
for the Capitol Police, in which he describes emails among senior
officials of the United States Capitol Police (USCP) in January 2021
that show warnings of possible January 6 protests that could lead to
serious disruptions at the U.S. Capitol.

In September, we received
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31273&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvamFudWFyeS02LXByb3Rlc3RvcnMtdGFyZ2V0ZWQvXXJlY29yZHM/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=3d2beaca2eca9b24c657a0f6595eb0bb7e63280b3ce6060ff463b171a63e024c
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=30173&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvamFudWFyeS02LXByb3Rlc3RvcnMtdGFyZ2V0ZWQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=aea6cd69ab6202353613d55e61ca57aa6dbaea0fa7f6a9fb55670a0e84ce80bf]
from
the Executive Office for United States Attorneys, a component of the
Department of Justice, in a FOIA lawsuit that detailed the extensive
apparatus the Biden Justice Department set up to investigate and

SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS SUE OVER PAYMENTS TO ‘BLACK/LATINX’ MEN
(‘TRANSGENDER WOMEN’)

The transgender extremists running San Francisco are illegally using
taxpayer money to hand out free cash to transgender individuals based
on race and sex in blatant violation of the state’s constitution.

We filed a lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31246&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3BoaWxsaXBzLWV0LWFsLXYtYnJlZWQtZXQtYWwtc2FuLWZyYW4tZ2lmdC1jb21wbGFpbnQtNjExOTE1Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=eb025825157c79884c51d150969e9801f4f724fdf6c05657a89eff230a47043a]
on behalf of San Francisco taxpayers over a city program that
discriminates in favor of biological black and Latino men who identify
as women in the distribution of tax money.

The taxpayer lawsuit was filed against San Francisco Mayor London
Breed, City Treasurer Jose Cisneros, the director of the city’s
Office of Transgender Initiatives, and City Administrator Carmen Chu
for violating the Equal Protection clause of the California
Constitution (_Phillips et al. v Breed et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31246&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3BoaWxsaXBzLWV0LWFsLXYtYnJlZWQtZXQtYWwtc2FuLWZyYW4tZ2lmdC1jb21wbGFpbnQtNjExOTE1Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=eb025825157c79884c51d150969e9801f4f724fdf6c05657a89eff230a47043a]
(No. 24-611915)).

Mayor Breed announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=30379&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3Nhbi1mcmFuY2lzY28tdHJhbnNnZW5kZXItbW9uZXktMjAyMy1wZ3MtMTItMTQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=9f4db7a75cf7cda63293cbfc0e533f2e7dfa2cca46fc59c21288b592657889d0]
the launch of the Guaranteed Income for Trans People (GIFT) program on
November 16, 2022. The mayor’s office stated in a press release that
the city will “provide low-income transgender San Franciscans with
$1,200 each month, up to 18 months to help address financial
insecurity within trans communities.”

According to our lawsuit:

> Applicants who do not identify as transgender, non-binary, gender
> non-conforming, or intersex are not eligible to participate in the
> GIFT program.
>
> Applicants are prioritized based on their biological sex and
> race/ethnicity. Biological males identifying as female are given
> preference over biological females identifying as male, and
> applicants identifying as Black or Latino are given preference over
> applicants identifying as other races/ethnicities.
The program began disbursing funds
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=30380&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ2lmdGluY29tZS5vcmcvZmFxP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=52f8fffcbcdc7e53d9749e070e2ef825ae6df15a0de6cd875a94b5d66d1a4ca7]
in January 2023. GIFT payments to these participants will continue
through June 2024.

Our taxpayer clients allege that San Francisco is discriminating in
violation of Article 1, Section 7 of the state constitution on three
counts:

> Transgender Status Discrimination:
> Plaintiffs contend that any expenditure of taxpayer funds or
> taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program is illegal ...
> because of the requirement that eligible participants be
> transgender, non-binary, gender nonconforming, or intersex is
> immediately suspect and presumptively invalid and cannot survive
> strict scrutiny review.

> Sex Discrimination:
> [P]laintiffs contend that any expenditure of taxpayer funds or
> taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program is illegal ...
> because the program grants preferential treatment to biological
> males who identify as females ...
>
> Race/Ethnicity Discrimination:
> Specifically, Plaintiffs contend that any expenditure of taxpayer
> funds or taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program is illegal
> ... because the program grants preferential treatment to persons who
> identify as Black or Latino ...
Our clients are asking for:

> A judgment declaring any and all expenditures of taxpayer funds and
> taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program to be illegal [and]
>
> An injunction permanently prohibiting Defendants from expending or
> causing the expenditure of taxpayer funds and taxpayer-financed
> resources on the GIFT program[.]

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=30858&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdmljdG9yeS1taW5uZWFwb2xpcy10ZWFjaGVycy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=48e8045b9fd5e4dcef643b8846376aa7aef1c869dc260c6945f0fac52aab9b6e]
our
historic lawsuit filed on behalf of a Minneapolis taxpayer over a
teachers’ contract that provides discriminatory job protections to
certain racial minorities to proceed.

In May 2022, we won a court battle
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=27378&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY291cnQtZGVjbGFyZXMtdW5jb25zdGl0dXRpb25hbC1jYWxpZm9ybmlhLWdlbmRlci1xdW90YS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=d770c41115ddb7b1066f8541d3a1a4ce9b39ae231a6571e1d78d1d99c6d29454]
against California’s gender quota law for corporate boards. The
verdict came after a 28-day trial. The verdict followed a similar
ruling
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=27068&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY2EtY291cnQtb3Bpbmlvbi1leHBsYWlucy1oaXN0b3JpYy1ydWxpbmcvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=10486a90f0d1b494d339528c63ba83606b69cc5f81843d2a0ba4c3dbae154057]
in our favor in April finding California’s race, ethnicity and LGBT
quotas for corporate boards unconstitutional.

The City of Asheville, NC, in January 2022 settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=26643&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctdmljdG9yeS1hc2hldmlsbGUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=a80dae9ae9cdd58fb0ef02a98266153d491f6ff898bcc838029d545a80ff6121]
our federal
civil rights lawsuit after agreeing to remove all racially
discriminatory provisions in a city-funded scholarship program.
Additionally, the city agreed to remove racially discriminatory
eligibility provisions in a related program that provides grants to
educators.

COURT HEARING IN SUIT SEEKING RELEASE OF ‘MANIFESTO’ OF NASHVILLE
SCHOOL SHOOTER

We were in court this week for a hearing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31238&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2hhbW1vbmQtdi1uYXNodmlsbGUtamFudWFyeS0yMDI0LWhlYXJpbmctb3JkZXItMDUzOC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=21f9d8da16ad34e7ee46e23fc127592a772aaac1875c157493f124d02c298487],
asking for the release of records related to the March 27, 2023,
shooting at The Covenant School in Tennessee, including the reported
“manifesto” written by the suspected female shooter. The hearing
was held before Chancellor I’Ashea Myles in the Chancery Court for
Davidson County, 20th Judicial District, in the public records lawsuit
against the Metropolitan Government of Nashville and Davidson County.

We filed our
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31283&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY292ZW5hbnQtc2Nob29sLXNob290ZXIvXWxhd3N1aXQ/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=538a5d870e1a567b7ba94c37e7a28d72819fe424832be4694e6ba6467854f002
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=29572&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY292ZW5hbnQtc2Nob29sLXNob290ZXIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6f9aa05e8e79d3ec973023f3c14fff8bfa2b7e52bcca70917c97d3d8be76c7c4]
on behalf of
retired Hamilton County Sheriff James Hammond and the Tennessee
Firearms Association, Inc. (“TFA”) (_Hammond et al. v.
Metropolitan Govt of Nashville et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=29573&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2hhbW1vbmQtdi1uYXNodmlsbGUtcGV0aXRpb24tZm9yLXJlY29yZHMvXV8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=0896c14701b3c4defd97e64cea295b42ee6b07938ec3c51f4049808ab51105f5
_(No. 23-0538-III)). The court later consolidated our lawsuit with
several others related to release of public records from The Covenant
School shooting.

Recently, petitioner Clata Brewer filed a motion
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31239&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2hhbW1vbmQtdi1uYXNodmlsbGUtYnJld2VyLWZpbGluZy0wNTM4Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=66b174bbdd322bc76e22b5ca21e560a795859035bb666b2c191512363dd01654]
asking the court to schedule a show cause hearing to finally rule on
whether to release the records and another motion asking the court to
unseal the initial log created by Metropolitan Nashville Police
Department (“MNPD”) describing records and materials in its
possession. Petitioner Brewer argues the log needs to be unsealed
because the current restrictions “interfere with the ability of the
parties to refer to various documents, identified in the Log ...”
Brewer explains “the information in the Log is necessary to allow an
unfettered presentation of evidence and arguments ...”

On April 13, 2023, the TFA submitted two open records requests. The
first asked the MNPD to produce copies of records or files and
inspection of “the ‘manifesto’ reportedly found in the home of
Audrey Elizabeth Hall on March 27, 2023.” The second asked MNPD
produce copies “all email communications of Metropolitan Nashville
Police Department officials regarding the mass shooting committed by
Audrey Elizabeth Hale on March 27, 2023, as well as MNPD officials’
text messages regarding the same, and copies of the ‘manifesto’
reported left by Audrey Elizabeth Hale in her vehicle.”

In a separate request on April 17, 2023, Sheriff Hammond asked that
the MNPD provide all criminal police reports documenting the incident.

The MNPD denied these requests citing without identifying any
underlying criminal case or even a potential defendant.

The Tennessee Public Records Act states
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=29574&F=T&&hash=4b9a6c10dce175e970a1b78bae539c3b8b006e918a2680beaa15ed837f0c4a71
“[a]ll state, county and municipal records shall ... be open for
personal inspection by any citizen of this state, and those in charge
of the records shall not refuse such right of inspection to any
citizen, unless otherwise provided by state law. See Tenn. Code Ann.
§ 10-7-503(a)(2)(A).” The lawsuit requests the court allow prompt
access to the requested public records.

On May 17, 2023, MNPD argued in court
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=29575&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2JyZXdlci1oYW1tb25kLXYtbmFzaHZpbGxlLXBvbGljZS1kZWNsYXJhdGlvbnMtMDU0Mi0wNTM4Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=3d6f27cb226ee13bd3797755540ec20542a186679fda93b5f2deb81ab9059594]
that it did not object to a release of the “redacted” manifesto,
but many other records are still under review. Additionally, the MNPD
stated the investigation could take approximately one year to
complete.

The resolution of the case has been delayed for disputes about whether
the school and school/victims parents could intervene and prevent the
release of the “manifesto” and other investigatory material.

John I. Harris III, Esq., of Schulman, LeRoy & Bennett, PC in
Nashville, TN, is assisting Judicial Watch with the lawsuit.

FEDERAL JUDGES GRANT ORAL ARGUMENT BASED ON LAWYERS’ SEX, RACE

Our federal judiciary is showing signs of “going woke,” as our
_Corruption Chronicles_ blog reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31257&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYXJndW1lbnQtYmFzZWQtb24tc2V4LXJhY2UvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=642b5fc176f921af8842d16f905f5a8b413b8ce50313bd455391b13c6d8aafc8].

> The robust woke movement gripping the nation’s private and public
> sectors is also threatening judicial impartiality with three federal
> judges enacting unlawful race and sex discrimination policies that
> grant oral argument in cases based on a lawyer’s gender and race.
> The goal is to give young women and minority attorneys greater
> opportunities to argue in court, according to the judges, who
> benefit from lifetime appointments. Two of the federal
> judges—Nancy J. Rosenstengel and Staci M. Yandle—were appointed
> by Barack Obama and one—David W. Dugan—is a Donald Trump
> appointee. All three sit on the U.S. District Court for the Southern
> District of Illinois.
>
> The unconventional policies adopted by the three jurists constitute
> judicial misconduct because they unlawfully discriminate, evidence
> judicial bias, undermine faith in the judiciary’s integrity, and
> violate the equal protection guarantee of the Fifth Amendment,
> according to a judicial misconduct complaint
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31284&F=T&&hash=ac84f746f902655621dbb1fb16042a42cae47412b156fbe720743080ac02ce3e]
> filed this month. To “encourage the participation of newer,
> female, and minority attorneys in proceedings,” the judges
> instituted a two-part process of discrimination, says the complaint
> filed by a conservative legal group. After a motion is fully briefed
> a party may alert the court that, if argument is granted, it intends
> to have a newer, female, or minority attorney argue the motion or a
> portion of the motion. The court will grant the request if
> practicable and “strongly consider allocating additional time for
> oral argument beyond what the Court may have otherwise allocated”
> but for the sex or race of the lawyer, the complaint reads. “The
> judges’ policies are essentially oral-argument affirmative action
> for lawyers,” according to the complaint, which points out that
> “the policies expressly reward female and minority lawyers.”
>
> In the two-page orders, which are identical, the three judges
> explain that they are cognizant of a growing trend in which fewer
> cases go to trial and in which there are generally fewer in-court
> advocacy opportunities. “This is especially true for newer
> attorneys (attorneys practicing for less than seven years) in
> general, and women and underrepresented minorities in particular,”
> the orders state. “Recognizing the importance of the development
> of future generations of practitioners through courtroom
> opportunities, the undersigned Judge encourages the participation of
> newer, female, and minority attorneys in proceedings in my
> courtroom; particularly with respect to oral argument on motions
> where said attorney drafted or contributed significantly to the
> briefing for the motion.” The reality is that the judges have a
> legal duty to grant oral argument requests based solely on the
> case’s legal merits and not the lawyer representing the parties
> engaged in the litigation.
>
> The judicial misconduct complaint stresses that few judicial acts
> are as confidence-shaking as an announcement by a federal judge that
> he or she will manage a case depending in part on the sex or race of
> a litigant’s attorney. “Judges do not promote public confidence
> in the integrity and impartiality of the judiciary when they
> announce policies expressly favoring persons with certain immutable
> characteristics,” the document states. “Public confidence in the
> judiciary is especially threatened when judges effect those policies
> through contemplated and actual exercise of the judicial power of
> the United States.” The document further points out that ratifying
> the discriminatory policies as standing orders creates an official
> acceptance of long-outlawed forms of discrimination in the federal
> judiciary.
>
> In a press release
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66860&L=31285&F=T&&hash=f8f0c7d9b4b8c5ac04827f10dfb96c085f40182d450ad3162c7be7478f42d306]
> announcing the judicial misconduct complaint, the Washington D.C.
> group, America First Legal, writes that all Americans deserve equal
> treatment and opportunity under the law and the legal profession has
> a special duty to serve as an example for the rest of society.
> “This is particularly true for federal judges, whose conduct must
> be beyond reproach–particularly when it comes to policies that
> overtly discriminate against attorneys appearing before them based
> on their race or sex.” The group adds that the Constitution
> forbids using sex and race preferences to provide parties with
> in-court argument time. The practice leaves less time available for
> lawyers of non-favored races or the wrong sex, which of course, is
> not legal and cheats both plaintiffs and defendants.
>
Until next week,

clean_body: Ashli Babbitt Update [INSIDE JW] Judicial Watch Sues for Details of DA Willis’ Hiring of Special Prosecutor [] We are in court fighting the lawfare being waged against former President Donald Trump. We filed a lawsuit [] against Fulton County, Georgia, for records of the hiring of Nathan Wade as a special prosecutor by District Attorney Fani Willis (Judicial Watch Inc. v. Fulton County, Georgia []). Wade was hired to pursue unprecedented criminal investigations and prosecutions against former President Trump and others over the 2020 election disputes. We sued after Fulton County failed to respond to a January 11, 2024, Georgia Open Records Act request for: All County public records, as defined in O.C.G.A.§ 50-18- 70(b)(2), related to the hiring/appointment/procurement of the professional services of Nathan Wade (or his law firm) as Fulton County Special Prosecutor. This includes any request for services/proposals, contracts, invoices, or correspondence (physical or electronic) related to his hiring/appointment/procurement. Any applicable procurement policies and procedures related to Mr. Wade’s hiring or appointment. Wade and Willis are allegedly involved in a romantic relationship. Earlier this month, records relating to Wade’s divorce were unsealed [] by a judge. They included evidence of his relationship with Willis such as “credit card charges for trips the pair took together to Florida and California.” His wife’s attorneys also asserted that Wade hid his true earnings, “totaling almost $700,000 for his work as special prosecutor.” Fani Willis’ politicized and unprecedented prosecution of former President Trump has been further compromised by credible allegations of personal corruption tied to the hiring of Nathan Wade as special prosecutor. That we had to file a lawsuit to try to get records about this scandal further suggests that there is something to hide. In October 2023, we sued [] the U.S. Department of Justice for records and communications between the Office of U.S. Special Counsel Jack Smith and the Fulton County, Georgia, District Attorney’s office regarding requests/receipt of federal funding/assistance in the investigation of former President Donald Trump and his 18 co-defendants in the Fulton County indictment [] of August 14, 2023. JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FBI FOR FILES ON ASHLI BABBITT – FAMILY DEMANDS RECORDS Ashli Babbitt was the only homicide victim on January 6, yet the FBI has been illicitly hiding its files on her from her family for a year. Why the cover-up? We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) [ [] on behalf of Aaron Babbitt, the late Ashli Elizabeth Babbitt’s husband, and Ashli Babbitt’s estate against the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) for all FBI files on Ashli Babbitt [ [ of Ashli Babbitt and Aaron Babbitt et al. v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 24-cv-0119)). Ashli Babbitt, an Air Force veteran, was shot and killed inside the U.S. Capitol by then-Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd on January 6, 2021. We sued in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of California to compel production of the FBI files on Ashli Babbitt and Aaron Babbitt after the FBI denied two February 27, 2023, FOIA requests to the FBI and subsequently failed to respond to an appeal of the denial for: All FBI files for Ashli Elizabeth Babbitt. Additional information: “Subject also used the names” Ashli Elizabeth McEntee and Ashli Elizabeth Pamatian;” and All FBI records, including main and cross-referenced files, related to Aaron Raymond Babbitt. Babbitt was a 35-year-old resident of San Diego, California, where she owned and operated a successful pool business with her husband Aaron. Ashli traveled alone from San Diego to Washington, D.C. to attend the Women for America First (aka Save America) rally on January 6, 2021, at the Ellipse. The shooting occurred at the east entrance to the Speaker’s Lobby. After demonstrators filled the hallway outside the lobby, two individuals in the crowded, tightly packed hallway struck and dislodged the glass panels in the lobby doors and the right door sidelight. Lt. Byrd, who is a U.S. Capitol Police commander and was the incident commander for the House on January 6, 2021, shot Ashli on sight as she raised herself up into the opening of the right door sidelight. Lt. Byrd later confessed that he shot Ashli before seeing her hands or assessing her intentions or even identifying her as female. Ashli was unarmed. Her hands were up in the air, empty, and in plain view of Lt. Byrd and other officers in the lobby. On January 5, 2024, we filed a wrongful death [ [] against the U.S. Government on behalf of the family of Ashli Babbitt (Estate of Ashli Babbitt and Aaron Babbitt, et al. v. United States of America [] (No. 3:24-cv-00033)). The lawsuit includes $30 million in claims against the U.S. Government for wrongful death, assault and battery, and various negligence issues. Describing Babbitt’s murder by Lt. Byrd, our complaint recounts: The facts speak truth. Ashli was ambushed when she was shot by Lt. Byrd. Multiple witnesses at the scene yelled, “you just murdered her.” Lt. Byrd was never charged or otherwise punished or disciplined for Ashli’s homicide. We are extensively investigating the events of January 6. In October 2023, we [ [] that we received the court-ordered [ [] of James W. Joyce, senior counsel in the Office of the General Counsel for the Capitol Police, in which he describes emails among senior officials of the United States Capitol Police (USCP) in January 2021 that show warnings of possible January 6 protests that could lead to serious disruptions at the U.S. Capitol. In September, we received [ [] from the Executive Office for United States Attorneys, a component of the Department of Justice, in a FOIA lawsuit that detailed the extensive apparatus the Biden Justice Department set up to investigate and prosecute January 6 protestors. A [ review of records [] from that lawsuit highlighted the [ declination memorandum [] justifying the decision not to prosecute U.S. Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd for the shooting death of Babbitt. In January 2023, [ [] from the Department of the Air Force, Joint Base Andrews, MD, showed U.S. Capitol Police Lieutenant Michael Byrd was housed at taxpayer expense at Joint Base Andrews after he shot and killed U.S. Air Force veteran Ashli Babbitt inside the U.S. Capitol on January 6, 2021. In November 2021, we [ [] multiple [ [], [ [ [] and [ records [] from the DC Metropolitan Police Department about the shooting death of Babbitt on January 6, 2021, in the U.S. Capitol Building. The records included a [ phone video [] of the shooting and an audio of a brief police interview of the shooter, Byrd. In October 2021, United States Park Police [ [] related to the January 6, 2021, demonstrations at the U.S. Capitol showed that on the day before the January 6 rally featuring President Trump, U.S. Park Police expected a “large portion” of the attendees to march to the U.S. Capitol and that the FBI was monitoring the January 6 demonstrations, including travel to the events by “subjects of interest.” SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS SUE OVER PAYMENTS TO ‘BLACK/LATINX’ MEN (‘TRANSGENDER WOMEN’) The transgender extremists running San Francisco are illegally using taxpayer money to hand out free cash to transgender individuals based on race and sex in blatant violation of the state’s constitution. We filed a lawsuit [] on behalf of San Francisco taxpayers over a city program that discriminates in favor of biological black and Latino men who identify as women in the distribution of tax money. The taxpayer lawsuit was filed against San Francisco Mayor London Breed, City Treasurer Jose Cisneros, the director of the city’s Office of Transgender Initiatives, and City Administrator Carmen Chu for violating the Equal Protection clause of the California Constitution (Phillips et al. v Breed et al. [] (No. 24-611915)). Mayor Breed announced [] the launch of the Guaranteed Income for Trans People (GIFT) program on November 16, 2022. The mayor’s office stated in a press release that the city will “provide low-income transgender San Franciscans with $1,200 each month, up to 18 months to help address financial insecurity within trans communities.” According to our lawsuit: Applicants who do not identify as transgender, non-binary, gender non-conforming, or intersex are not eligible to participate in the GIFT program. Applicants are prioritized based on their biological sex and race/ethnicity. Biological males identifying as female are given preference over biological females identifying as male, and applicants identifying as Black or Latino are given preference over applicants identifying as other races/ethnicities. The program began disbursing funds [] in January 2023. GIFT payments to these participants will continue through June 2024. Our taxpayer clients allege that San Francisco is discriminating in violation of Article 1, Section 7 of the state constitution on three counts: Transgender Status Discrimination: Plaintiffs contend that any expenditure of taxpayer funds or taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program is illegal ... because of the requirement that eligible participants be transgender, non-binary, gender nonconforming, or intersex is immediately suspect and presumptively invalid and cannot survive strict scrutiny review. Sex Discrimination: [P]laintiffs contend that any expenditure of taxpayer funds or taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program is illegal ... because the program grants preferential treatment to biological males who identify as females ... Race/Ethnicity Discrimination: Specifically, Plaintiffs contend that any expenditure of taxpayer funds or taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program is illegal ... because the program grants preferential treatment to persons who identify as Black or Latino ... Our clients are asking for: A judgment declaring any and all expenditures of taxpayer funds and taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program to be illegal [and] An injunction permanently prohibiting Defendants from expending or causing the expenditure of taxpayer funds and taxpayer-financed resources on the GIFT program[.] In October 2023, our open records request forced the release of records [] from the City of San Francisco showing the city prioritized tax money for black and Latino transgenders (biological men) in the (GIFT []) program, which also allowed illegal aliens to apply; allowed people who “engage in survival sex trades” to apply; and the use of the funds by participants was virtually unrestricted. In December 2023, the Minnesota Court of Appeals reversed the trial court’s ruling and allowed [] our historic lawsuit filed on behalf of a Minneapolis taxpayer over a teachers’ contract that provides discriminatory job protections to certain racial minorities to proceed. In May 2022, we won a court battle [] against California’s gender quota law for corporate boards. The verdict came after a 28-day trial. The verdict followed a similar ruling [] in our favor in April finding California’s race, ethnicity and LGBT quotas for corporate boards unconstitutional. The City of Asheville, NC, in January 2022 settled [] our federal civil rights lawsuit after agreeing to remove all racially discriminatory provisions in a city-funded scholarship program. Additionally, the city agreed to remove racially discriminatory eligibility provisions in a related program that provides grants to educators. COURT HEARING IN SUIT SEEKING RELEASE OF ‘MANIFESTO’ OF NASHVILLE SCHOOL SHOOTER We were in court this week for a hearing [], asking for the release of records related to the March 27, 2023, shooting at The Covenant School in Tennessee, including the reported “manifesto” written by the suspected female shooter. The hearing was held before Chancellor I’Ashea Myles in the Chancery Court for Davidson County, 20th Judicial District, in the public records lawsuit against the Metropolitan Government of Nashville and Davidson County. We filed our [ [] on behalf of retired Hamilton County Sheriff James Hammond and the Tennessee Firearms Association, Inc. (“TFA”) (Hammond et al. v. Metropolitan Govt of Nashville et al. [ (No. 23-0538-III)). The court later consolidated our lawsuit with several others related to release of public records from The Covenant School shooting. Recently, petitioner Clata Brewer filed a motion [] asking the court to schedule a show cause hearing to finally rule on whether to release the records and another motion asking the court to unseal the initial log created by Metropolitan Nashville Police Department (“MNPD”) describing records and materials in its possession. Petitioner Brewer argues the log needs to be unsealed because the current restrictions “interfere with the ability of the parties to refer to various documents, identified in the Log ...” Brewer explains “the information in the Log is necessary to allow an unfettered presentation of evidence and arguments ...” On April 13, 2023, the TFA submitted two open records requests. The first asked the MNPD to produce copies of records or files and inspection of “the ‘manifesto’ reportedly found in the home of Audrey Elizabeth Hall on March 27, 2023.” The second asked MNPD produce copies “all email communications of Metropolitan Nashville Police Department officials regarding the mass shooting committed by Audrey Elizabeth Hale on March 27, 2023, as well as MNPD officials’ text messages regarding the same, and copies of the ‘manifesto’ reported left by Audrey Elizabeth Hale in her vehicle.” In a separate request on April 17, 2023, Sheriff Hammond asked that the MNPD provide all criminal police reports documenting the incident. The MNPD denied these requests citing without identifying any underlying criminal case or even a potential defendant. The Tennessee Public Records Act states [ “[a]ll state, county and municipal records shall ... be open for personal inspection by any citizen of this state, and those in charge of the records shall not refuse such right of inspection to any citizen, unless otherwise provided by state law. See Tenn. Code Ann. § 10-7-503(a)(2)(A).” The lawsuit requests the court allow prompt access to the requested public records. On May 17, 2023, MNPD argued in court [] that it did not object to a release of the “redacted” manifesto, but many other records are still under review. Additionally, the MNPD stated the investigation could take approximately one year to complete. The resolution of the case has been delayed for disputes about whether the school and school/victims parents could intervene and prevent the release of the “manifesto” and other investigatory material. John I. Harris III, Esq., of Schulman, LeRoy & Bennett, PC in Nashville, TN, is assisting Judicial Watch with the lawsuit. FEDERAL JUDGES GRANT ORAL ARGUMENT BASED ON LAWYERS’ SEX, RACE Our federal judiciary is showing signs of “going woke,” as our Corruption Chronicles blog reports []. The robust woke movement gripping the nation’s private and public sectors is also threatening judicial impartiality with three federal judges enacting unlawful race and sex discrimination policies that grant oral argument in cases based on a lawyer’s gender and race. The goal is to give young women and minority attorneys greater opportunities to argue in court, according to the judges, who benefit from lifetime appointments. Two of the federal judges—Nancy J. Rosenstengel and Staci M. Yandle—were appointed by Barack Obama and one—David W. Dugan—is a Donald Trump appointee. All three sit on the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Illinois. The unconventional policies adopted by the three jurists constitute judicial misconduct because they unlawfully discriminate, evidence judicial bias, undermine faith in the judiciary’s integrity, and violate the equal protection guarantee of the Fifth Amendment, according to a judicial misconduct complaint [] filed this month. To “encourage the participation of newer, female, and minority attorneys in proceedings,” the judges instituted a two-part process of discrimination, says the complaint filed by a conservative legal group. After a motion is fully briefed a party may alert the court that, if argument is granted, it intends to have a newer, female, or minority attorney argue the motion or a portion of the motion. The court will grant the request if practicable and “strongly consider allocating additional time for oral argument beyond what the Court may have otherwise allocated” but for the sex or race of the lawyer, the complaint reads. “The judges’ policies are essentially oral-argument affirmative action for lawyers,” according to the complaint, which points out that “the policies expressly reward female and minority lawyers.” In the two-page orders, which are identical, the three judges explain that they are cognizant of a growing trend in which fewer cases go to trial and in which there are generally fewer in-court advocacy opportunities. “This is especially true for newer attorneys (attorneys practicing for less than seven years) in general, and women and underrepresented minorities in particular,” the orders state. “Recognizing the importance of the development of future generations of practitioners through courtroom opportunities, the undersigned Judge encourages the participation of newer, female, and minority attorneys in proceedings in my courtroom; particularly with respect to oral argument on motions where said attorney drafted or contributed significantly to the briefing for the motion.” The reality is that the judges have a legal duty to grant oral argument requests based solely on the case’s legal merits and not the lawyer representing the parties engaged in the litigation. The judicial misconduct complaint stresses that few judicial acts are as confidence-shaking as an announcement by a federal judge that he or she will manage a case depending in part on the sex or race of a litigant’s attorney. “Judges do not promote public confidence in the integrity and impartiality of the judiciary when they announce policies expressly favoring persons with certain immutable characteristics,” the document states. “Public confidence in the judiciary is especially threatened when judges effect those policies through contemplated and actual exercise of the judicial power of the United States.” The document further points out that ratifying the discriminatory policies as standing orders creates an official acceptance of long-outlawed forms of discrimination in the federal judiciary. In a press release [] announcing the judicial misconduct complaint, the Washington D.C. group, America First Legal, writes that all Americans deserve equal treatment and opportunity under the law and the legal profession has a special duty to serve as an example for the rest of society. “This is particularly true for federal judges, whose conduct must be beyond reproach–particularly when it comes to policies that overtly discriminate against attorneys appearing before them based on their race or sex.” The group adds that the Constitution forbids using sex and race preferences to provide parties with in-court argument time. The practice leaves less time available for lawyers of non-favored races or the wrong sex, which of course, is not legal and cheats both plaintiffs and defendants. Until next week, [Contribute] [] [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2024, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |
